title: clarisia biflora subsp. mexicana
intro: Clarisia biflora subsp. mexicana, also known as Mexican Clarisia, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Clusiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The plant is said to have numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. Clarisia biflora subsp. mexicana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine in Mexico and other parts of Latin America.
